sources.label: NHS service manual how we write
borrowed: Keep patient wording factual, neutral, and plain.
Prefer short sentences and active voice.
Avoid technical transport or internal workflow jargon.
rejected: Using process-heavy staff phrasing for patient next-step copy.

sources.label: NHS service manual confirmation page
borrowed: Use a confirmation-led top block only when a transactional step is genuinely complete.
Keep what happens next directly under the confirmation state.
rejected: Using a calm completion panel before the pharmacy outcome is actually settled.

sources.label: NHS England Pharmacy First
borrowed: Position pharmacy as a route for advice, treatment, and safety-netting rather than an appointment booking flow.
Keep the patient-safe explanation that the pharmacy may contact the patient and will notify the GP after consultation.
rejected: Presenting the pharmacy route as a booked slot or a detached appointment journey.

sources.label: WAI-ARIA APG alert pattern
borrowed: Use alert treatment only for important, time-sensitive repair or urgent states.
Do not move focus when announcing those states.
rejected: Turning every status change into an interruptive alert.

sources.label: WAI-ARIA APG disclosure pattern
borrowed: Use button-based disclosures with aria-expanded and aria-controls for the tracker detail rows.
rejected: Using hover-only or non-button affordances for status detail.

sources.label: WAI-ARIA APG landmark regions
borrowed: Keep one named navigation landmark for the route family.
Reserve region semantics for labeled, navigable sections.
rejected: Sprinkling unlabeled regions across the shell.

sources.label: WAI-ARIA 1.2 status role
borrowed: Use status semantics for advisory review content that should not take focus.
Keep status announcements polite and atomic.
rejected: Using status semantics for urgent-action states that need alert treatment.

sources.label: Playwright accessibility testing
borrowed: Pair automated accessibility checks with manual landmark and role assertions.
rejected: Treating one automated snapshot as complete accessibility coverage.

sources.label: Playwright emulation
borrowed: Keep mobile-width and reduced-motion proofs in the targeted browser battery.
rejected: Relying on desktop-only screenshots for route-family regressions.

sources.label: Linear design refresh
borrowed: Let structure be felt rather than loudly drawn.
Keep support details visually quieter than the current task surface.
rejected: Over-borded dense cards that compete with the main patient instruction content.
